blocks.heading: The shared block‚ÜíHTML rendering engine every generator builds on ‚Äî a typed `Block` vocabulary, per-type renderers and helpers, and an `assemble()` that turns a `Block[]` into one self-contained HTML document.

rows.value: `src` (the engine modules) + `src/renderers/*` (one renderer per block type).

Owns the `Block` union and the shared render primitives. `doc` feeds its `Block[]` straight to `assemble()`; recap, spec, and atlas ship their own shells that reuse core's renderers, markdown, and escaping.

bigIdea.label: The load-bearing idea
bigIdea.line: "One Block vocabulary and one set of render primitives ‚Äî every product's page is built from the same core pieces."
bigIdea.sub: A product's job is to build blocks ‚Äî diagrams, diffs, prose, file trees, annotated code. The plain-doc path is `assemble()`: it walks the `Block[]`, dispatches each block to its renderer by a `switch (b.type)`, inlines the CSS + viewer JS, and returns one portable HTML string. The recap, spec, and atlas shells live in their own domains but build on the same union, renderers, markdown, and escaping ‚Äî add a primitive once here and every generator can use it.

blocks.title: The pieces
blocks.intro: A typed block model at the center, one `assemble()` that walks it into an HTML shell, a fan-out of per-type renderers, and three helpers (highlight, lint, version). Tap a card to jump to its full section.

cards.name: block-model
cards.purpose: The `Block` discriminated union ‚Äî the contract between products and the engine. Every renderable thing (diagram, schema, diff, prose, api, file-tree, annotated-code, questions, group, tabs, overview) is one variant, keyed by `type`.

cards.name: assemble + shell
cards.purpose: The pipeline: validate unique ids, render diagrams up front, dispatch each block to its renderer, then wrap the fragments in an HTML shell with inlined CSS + viewer JS. `html.ts` supplies the escaping and safe-href guards the whole engine shares.

cards.name: renderers
cards.purpose: Nine renderer modules: seven pure `Block ‚Üí HTML` functions (prose, diff, api, file-tree, annotated-code, questions, overview), plus the diagram color-key painter and the shared markdown substrate the prose/diff/overview renderers lean on. `assemble`'s `switch` calls the right one; groups and tabs recurse back through `assemble`.

cards.name: highlight
cards.purpose: Shiki-backed syntax highlighting behind a lazy singleton. `highlightCode` returns a full `<pre>`; `highlightLines` returns per-line inner HTML for the gutter-aligned diff and annotated-code grids. Both degrade to escaped plaintext on any failure.

cards.name: lint
cards.purpose: Non-blocking authoring guards. Walks the `Block[]` and warns ‚Äî never throws ‚Äî when a group lacks a description, a diff description is a wall of text, a diagram applies no color, an editable kind drops its mermaid, or there are too many / oversize diagrams.

cards.name: version
cards.purpose: The provenance stamp: `visual-skills@<git-sha>[-dirty] ¬∑ <ISO timestamp>`, stamped into the page `<meta generator>` and footer. Falls back to the package.json version, then `"unknown"`, when git is unavailable.

blocks.title: How a document is assembled
blocks.intro: The `doc` product hands `assemble()` a `Block[]` (recap, spec, and atlas run their own shells on the same pieces). It validates ids, renders every diagram up front via the `diagrams` domain, then dispatches each block to its renderer and wraps the result in the HTML shell it builds ‚Äî `escapeHtml` from `html.ts`, CSS + viewer JS inlined. `highlight` and `lint` are helpers along the way; `version` stamps the page.

blocks.title: The pieces, in depth
blocks.intro: A full section per piece ‚Äî what it does, how it works, its files and exports, and how it connects to the rest of the engine and the products that call it.

components.name: block-model
detail: `Block` is a discriminated union keyed on `type` ‚Äî `diagram`, `schema`, `api`, `file-tree`, `diff`, `prose`, `annotated-code`, `questions`, `group`, `tabs`, `overview`. This is the entire contract between a product and the engine: a generator never touches HTML, it only constructs typed blocks. `assemble`'s exhaustive `switch (b.type)` is checked against this union, so adding a variant is a compile error until a renderer handles it.

Two variants are containers that nest **one level deep**: `group` (a labelled `<details>` of child blocks) and `tabs` (a CSS-only switcher, max 6 tabs). `diff` and `overview` may each carry an optional embedded `diagram`. The only logic here is `isDiagramBlock`, the type guard `assemble` uses to find every `diagram`/`schema` block ‚Äî including those nested inside groups, tabs, diffs, and overviews ‚Äî so they can be rendered through the diagrams domain up front.

files.name: blocks.ts
files.desc: The `Block` union, every variant interface, and the `isDiagramBlock` guard.

exports.name: Block
exports.desc: The discriminated union every product builds and `assemble` walks.

exports.name: DiagramBlock
exports.desc: The `type: "diagram"` variant ‚Äî required d2 source + optional mermaid.

exports.name: DiffBlock
exports.desc: The `type: "diff"` variant ‚Äî path, description, optional diagram, hunks.

exports.name: ProseBlock
exports.desc: The `type: "prose"` variant ‚Äî a markdown section.

exports.name: ApiBlock
exports.desc: The `type: "api"` variant ‚Äî tRPC procedure rows (one interface per variant, keyed by `type`).

exports.name: isDiagramBlock()
exports.desc: Type guard ‚Äî is this block rendered through the d2/mermaid diagram path?

connections.body: [recap](../domain-recap/domain-recap.html) and [authoring](../domain-authoring/domain-authoring.html)'s `doc` ‚Äî each assembles a full `Block[]`; the spec and atlas shells compile their diagrams down to `DiagramBlock`
atlas-page blocks (`src/atlas-blocks.ts`) compile down to `DiagramBlock` via this same model

components.name: assemble + shell
detail: `assemble(blocks, opts)` is the plain-document pipeline ‚Äî the `doc` generator calls it directly, while the recap/spec/atlas shells reuse its pieces rather than the function itself. It first asserts every block id is unique recursively (anchors and in-page `#cross-links` depend on it), runs `lintBlocks` through `opts.onWarn`, then collects every diagram/schema block ‚Äî including nested ones ‚Äî and renders them up front through the diagrams domain, keyed by id. It then maps each top-level block to HTML via `renderBlock`, a `switch (b.type)` that calls the matching renderer; `group` and `tabs` recurse. Finally it inlines `assets/template.css` and `assets/viewer.js`, stamps an optional `<meta generator>` and footer, and returns a single self-contained HTML string ‚Äî no external assets, by design.

`html.ts` is the tiny shared safety layer: `escapeHtml` (used by every renderer for any text that reaches HTML) and `SAFE_HREF`, the regex that allows only `#fragment` and `http(s)://` links so a `javascript:`/`data:` URL can't slip through the overview and review link renderers. `assemble` also injects each block's id as an anchor on its `<section>` so the cross-links resolve.

files.name: assemble.ts
files.desc: Id validation, diagram pre-render, the `renderBlock` dispatch switch, and the HTML shell.

files.name: html.ts
files.desc: `escapeHtml` and the `SAFE_HREF` link-scheme allowlist shared across renderers.

exports.name: assemble()
exports.desc: ‚Üí `Promise<string>` ‚Äî `Block[]` + opts to one self-contained HTML document.

exports.name: AssembleOpts
exports.desc: Title, source, optional status banner, outDir, excalidraw, onWarn, generator.

exports.name: DocStatus
exports.desc: The optional green/yellow/red status banner shown in the header.

exports.name: escapeHtml()
exports.desc: HTML-text escaping every renderer reuses.

exports.name: SAFE_HREF
exports.desc: The `#fragment` / `http(s)://` link-scheme guard.

connections.body: [authoring](../domain-authoring/domain-authoring.html)'s `bin/doc` ‚Äî recap, spec, and atlas ship their own shells (`assemble-review` / `assemble-spec` / `assemble-atlas`) that reuse core's renderers and helpers instead

components.name: renderers
detail: Nine modules: seven per-block-type functions ‚Äî `renderProse`, `renderDiff`, `renderApi`, `renderFileTree`, `renderAnnotatedCode`, `renderOverview`, `renderQuestions` ‚Äî plus `renderLegend` (the diagram color key) and the shared `renderMarkdown`. Each block renderer takes its block (some plus an `onWarn` or a pre-rendered extra) and returns an HTML fragment wrapped in a `vs-block` section; `assemble` adds the anchor and stitches them together. There is no shared base class ‚Äî the union + the dispatch switch are the only structure.

`markdown.ts` is the substrate: `renderMarkdown` parses GitHub-flavored markdown, highlights fenced code via the highlight module, and runs the result through `sanitize-html` with a strict tag/attribute allowlist; `renderInlineMarkdown` does the one-line variant for headlines and bullets. `prose`, `diff`, and `overview` all build on it. `diff` and `annotated-code` use `highlightLines` to align highlighted code to a gutter; `file-tree` collapses single-child directory chains and links filenames back to their diff blocks; `legend` paints the auto-derived diagram color key.

files.name: markdown.ts
files.desc: `renderMarkdown` / `renderInlineMarkdown` ‚Äî GFM + Shiki + sanitize-html. The shared substrate.

files.name: prose.ts
files.desc: `renderProse` ‚Äî a markdown prose body with optional heading.

files.name: annotated-code.ts
files.desc: `renderAnnotatedCode` ‚Äî a code grid with per-line margin notes, built on `highlightLines`.

files.name: file-tree.ts
files.desc: `renderFileTree` ‚Äî the change tree, filenames linked to their diffs.

exports.name: renderMarkdown()
exports.desc: Sanitized GFM ‚Üí HTML; the substrate the prose/diff/overview renderers reuse.

exports.name: renderInlineMarkdown()
exports.desc: The one-line sanitized variant for headlines and bullets.

exports.name: renderProse()
exports.desc: Markdown prose section with optional heading.

exports.name: renderDiff()
exports.desc: Gutter-aligned diff hunks behind a `<details>` with +/‚àí stats.

exports.name: renderApi()
exports.desc: The tRPC procedure table.

exports.name: renderFileTree()
exports.desc: The change tree, filenames linked to their diff blocks.

exports.name: renderOverview()
exports.desc: TL;DR headline + key points with safe-href arrow links.

exports.name: renderQuestions()
exports.desc: Open-question cards with recommended defaults.

exports.name: renderAnnotatedCode()
exports.desc: Code listing with per-line margin notes.

exports.name: renderLegend()
exports.desc: The diagram color key ‚Äî role swatches from the diagrams palette.

connections.body: [assemble()](#c-assemble) ‚Äî one per block `type` from the dispatch switch
[highlight](#c-highlight) for code spans; [html.ts](#c-assemble) `escapeHtml` for all text
[diagrams](../domain-diagrams/domain-diagrams.html) ‚Äî `renderLegend` paints swatches from its `PALETTE` / role labels

the [recap](../domain-recap/domain-recap.html), [atlas](../domain-atlas/domain-atlas.html), and [authoring](../domain-authoring/domain-authoring.html) shells ‚Äî markdown everywhere; prose, questions, and annotated-code in the recap sections

components.name: highlight
detail: A thin wrapper over Shiki (`github-light` theme, a fixed set of loaded languages) behind a lazy promise singleton, so the highlighter is created at most once per process. `highlightCode` returns a complete Shiki `<pre>` for fenced markdown code; `highlightLines` returns the inner HTML of each source line so `diff` and `annotated-code` can interleave code with gutters and per-line notes. `langFromPath` maps a file extension to a Shiki language id.

Every path is defensive: an unloaded language, a Shiki error, or a line-count mismatch degrades to escaped plaintext (and an `onWarn` note) rather than throwing ‚Äî a highlighting failure must never break a document.

exports.name: highlightCode()
exports.desc: ‚Üí a full highlighted `<pre>`, or escaped plain `<pre>` on failure.

exports.name: highlightLines()
exports.desc: ‚Üí per-line inner HTML (or `null`) for gutter-aligned grids.

exports.name: langFromPath()
exports.desc: File extension ‚Üí Shiki language id; unknown ‚Üí `"text"`.

connections.body: [renderers](#c-renderers) ‚Äî markdown fenced code, diff hunks, annotated code
escaped plaintext via [html.ts](#c-assemble) `escapeHtml` on any failure

components.name: lint
detail: `lintBlocks(blocks)` returns authoring warnings in document order ‚Äî it never throws and never blocks rendering. `assemble` pipes them to `opts.onWarn`. The guards nudge quality: a `group` with no description, a `diff` description that is a long single paragraph (a wall of text), a `diagram` that applies no semantic color, an editable-eligible diagram kind that dropped its mermaid source (silently losing the Excalidraw upgrade), and too-many / oversize-edge diagrams overall.

It walks the same nested structure `assemble` does ‚Äî groups, tabs, embedded diff/overview diagrams ‚Äî using the diagram-color helpers to detect which roles a d2/mermaid source applies. The intent is to catch the mistakes that make a document read worse without making the build fail.

files.name: lint-blocks.ts
files.desc: The group / diff-prose / diagram authoring guards, recursing through nested blocks.

exports.name: lintBlocks()
exports.desc: ‚Üí `string[]` of non-blocking authoring warnings in document order.

connections.body: diagram color roles via the [diagrams](../domain-diagrams/domain-diagrams.html) color helpers

components.name: version
detail: `generatorStamp()` produces the provenance string `visual-skills@<git-short-sha>[-dirty] ¬∑ <ISO timestamp>`. It shells out to git for the short SHA and a porcelain status (the `-dirty` suffix when the tree has uncommitted changes); if git is unavailable it falls back to the package.json version, then to `"unknown"`. The `recap` and `doc` CLIs pass the result as `opts.generator`, which the shell writes into the page `<meta generator>` and footer so any saved document records exactly which build produced it (the spec and atlas CLIs default to a static skill label instead).

files.name: version.ts
files.desc: `generatorStamp` ‚Äî git SHA / dirty / timestamp with package.json fallback.

exports.name: generatorStamp()
exports.desc: ‚Üí `Promise<string>` provenance stamp for the page meta + footer.

blocks.title: The block model it owns
blocks.intro: Core owns the `Block` discriminated union ‚Äî the type model every product builds against and the engine renders. These are the variants it defines.

rows.name: Block
rows.desc: The discriminated union itself ‚Äî the contract between products and `assemble()`.

rows.name: DiagramBlock ¬∑ SchemaBlock
rows.desc: A d2 (+ optional mermaid) diagram, and the d2-only ERD schema variant.

rows.name: ApiBlock
rows.desc: A tRPC procedure table ‚Äî name, kind, auth, input, and change marker per row.

rows.name: DiffBlock
rows.desc: A file diff: path, optional markdown description, optional embedded diagram, and hunks.

rows.name: FileTreeBlock
rows.desc: A change tree of `FileChange` entries, linkable back to their diff blocks.

rows.name: ProseBlock ¬∑ OverviewBlock
rows.desc: A markdown prose section, and the TL;DR headline + key points (optional lead diagram, plus optional review facets/risk).

rows.name: AnnotatedCodeBlock
rows.desc: A code listing with per-line margin notes, gutter-aligned to highlighted source.

rows.name: QuestionsBlock
rows.desc: Open-question cards, each with a recommended default.

rows.name: GroupBlock ¬∑ TabsBlock
rows.desc: The two one-level-deep containers: a labelled `<details>` group and a CSS-only tab switcher.

blocks.note: All HTML is produced at render time and inlined ‚Äî core owns no persisted data, only the in-memory block types and the document string `assemble()` returns.

blocks.title: Seams
blocks.intro: A small typed surface: products build blocks against the `Block` union and render through core's primitives; core in turn calls the diagrams domain to render every diagram up front.
blocks.note: [Authoring](../domain-authoring/domain-authoring.html)'s `doc` hands a `Block[]` to `assemble()`; [recap](../domain-recap/domain-recap.html), [atlas](../domain-atlas/domain-atlas.html), and the spec side of authoring ship their own shells that reuse core's `Block` union, renderers, and helpers. Core's only dependency is [diagrams](../domain-diagrams/domain-diagrams.html): `assemble` renders every diagram/schema block through it before dispatching the rest.
